theme: syncing a relay-clock network with rationed cable upgrades
title: Relay-Clock Sync: Rationed Cable Upgrades Under Node Caps
strategies: trivial: leave every cable at its installed base weight 1, spend no budget (reproduces the checker's do-nothing floor)

greedy: compute the Fiedler vector once on the base network, rank cables by that static sensitivity, and fill them in that fixed order to their cap/budget limit -- fixates on the visible bottleneck cable, which is capped almost exactly at its current load, and wastes or crudely dumps the remainder

strong: cap-aware coordinate ascent -- spend the budget in small increments, always on the currently highest-Fiedler-sensitivity cable with spare node-cap room, recomputing the Fiedler vector after every increment so the spend spills onto cap-free parallel detour cables once the bottleneck saturates
invalid: dumps the whole budget onto one cable, blowing through its node's weighted-degree cap
